title: Sum of Subsequence Widths
statement:
The **width** of a subsequence is the difference between its maximum and minimum element. Find the sum of widths of all non-empty subsequences of the array. Return the answer mod 10⁹+7.

**Input:** First line: n. Second line: n space-separated integers.
**Output:** Sum of widths mod 10⁹+7.

examples.explanation: Sort: 1,2,3. All non-empty subsequences: single elements (width 0), pairs: (1,2)=1,(1,3)=2,(2,3)=1, triple (1,2,3)=2. Sum=0+0+0+1+2+1+2=6.
Only one element, only one subsequence of width 0.
